# Battery energy storage system

Appearance

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Energy storage system using electrochemical secondary cells

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Overhead_View_of_Tehachapi_Energy_Storage_Project%2C_Tehachapi%2C_CA.png/330px-Overhead_View_of_Tehachapi_Energy_Storage_Project%2C_Tehachapi%2C_CA.png)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Overhead_View_of_Tehachapi_Energy_Storage_Project,_Tehachapi,_CA.png)

[Tehachapi Energy Storage Project](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tehachapi_Energy_Storage_Project "Tehachapi Energy Storage Project"), Tehachapi, California

A **battery energy storage system** (**BESS**), **battery storage power station**, **battery energy grid storage** (**BEGS**) or **battery grid storage** is a type of [energy storage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Energy_storage "Energy storage") technology that uses a group of [batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rechargeable_battery "Rechargeable battery") in the grid to store [electrical energy](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electrical_energy "Electrical energy"). Battery storage is the fastest responding [dispatchable source of power](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dispatchable_generation "Dispatchable generation") on [electric grids](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electric_grid "Electric grid"), and it is used to stabilise those grids, as battery storage can transition from standby to full power in under a second to deal with [grid contingencies](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Contingency_(electrical_grid) "Contingency (electrical grid)").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Battery energy storage systems are generally designed to deliver their full rated power for durations ranging from 1 to 4 hours, with emerging technologies extending this to longer durations to meet evolving grid demands.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Battery storage can be used for short-term [peak power](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peaking_power_plant "Peaking power plant")[[3]](#cite_note-GTM-3) demand and for [ancillary services](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancillary_services "Ancillary services"), such as providing [operating reserve](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Operating_reserve "Operating reserve") and [frequency control](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequency_control "Frequency control") to minimize the chance of [power outages](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_outage "Power outage").

They are often installed at, or close to, other active or disused power stations and may share the same grid connection to reduce costs. Since battery storage plants require no deliveries of fuel, are compact compared to generating stations and have no chimneys or large cooling systems, they can be rapidly installed and placed if necessary within urban areas, close to customer load, or even inside customer premises.

As of 2021, the power and capacity of the largest individual battery storage system is an [order of magnitude](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Order_of_magnitude "Order of magnitude") less than that of the largest [pumped-storage power plants](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pumped-storage_power_plant "Pumped-storage power plant"), the most common form of [grid energy storage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grid_energy_storage "Grid energy storage"). For example, the [Bath County Pumped Storage Station](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bath_County_Pumped_Storage_Station "Bath County Pumped Storage Station"), the second largest in the world, can store 24 GWh of electricity and dispatch 3 GW while the first phase of [Vistra Energy](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vistra_Energy "Vistra Energy")'s Moss Landing Energy Storage Facility can store 1.2 GWh and dispatch 300 MW.[[4]](#cite_note-4) However, grid batteries do not have to be large — a high number of smaller ones (often as [hybrid power](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hybrid_power "Hybrid power")) can be widely deployed across a grid for greater redundancy and large overall capacity. By 2025, global power capacity was 267 GW with 610 GWh energy capacity.[[5]](#cite_note-tris2025-5)

As of 2019, battery power storage is typically cheaper than [open cycle gas turbine](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_cycle_gas_turbine "Open cycle gas turbine") power for use up to two hours, and there was around 365 GWh of battery storage deployed worldwide, growing rapidly.[[6]](#cite_note-fallinglcoe-6) [Levelized cost of storage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Levelized_cost_of_storage "Levelized cost of storage") (LCOS) has fallen rapidly. From 2014 to 2024, cost halving time was 4.1 years.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The price was US$150 per MWh in 2020,[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) and further reduced to US$117 by 2023.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Construction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Construction")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cc/Datacenter_Backup_Batteries.jpg/250px-Datacenter_Backup_Batteries.jpg)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Datacenter_Backup_Batteries.jpg)

A rechargeable battery bank used in a data center

 [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Invenergy_Beech_Ridge_Energy_Storage_System.jpg/250px-Invenergy_Beech_Ridge_Energy_Storage_System.jpg)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Invenergy_Beech_Ridge_Energy_Storage_System.jpg)

[Lithium iron phosphate battery](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium_iron_phosphate_battery "Lithium iron phosphate battery") modules packaged in shipping containers installed at [Beech Ridge Energy Storage System](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beech_Ridge_Wind_Farm "Beech Ridge Wind Farm") in West Virginia[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Battery storage power plants and [uninterruptible power supplies](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uninterruptible_power_supplies "Uninterruptible power supplies") (UPS) are comparable in technology and function. However, battery storage power plants are larger.

For safety and security, the actual batteries are housed in their own structures, like warehouses or containers. As with a UPS, one concern is that electrochemical energy is stored or emitted in the form of [direct current](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Direct_current "Direct current") (DC), while electric power networks are usually operated with [alternating current](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alternating_current "Alternating current") (AC). For this reason, additional [inverters](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inverter "Inverter") are needed to connect the battery storage power plants to the high voltage network. This kind of power electronics include [gate turn-off thyristor](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gate_turn-off_thyristor "Gate turn-off thyristor"), commonly used in [high-voltage direct current](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-voltage_direct_current "High-voltage direct current") (HVDC) transmission.

Various accumulator systems may be used depending on the power-to-energy ratio, the expected lifetime and the costs. In the 1980s, lead-acid batteries were used for the first battery-storage power plants. During the next few decades, nickel–cadmium, nickel-hydride and sodium–sulfur batteries were increasingly used.[[14]](#cite_note-elsoc1-14) Since 2010, more and more utility-scale battery storage plants rely on [lithium-ion batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium-ion_batteries "Lithium-ion batteries"), as a result of their fast cost decrease combined with increased energy and power, caused by the electric automotive industry, and they are now the main type used. Batteries are becoming increasingly diverse, optimized for different aspects of customer requirements such as cost, safety or durability.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

A 4-hour [flow](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flow_battery "Flow battery") [vanadium redox battery](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vanadium_redox_battery "Vanadium redox battery") at 175 MW / 700 MWh opened in 2024.[[16]](#cite_note-16) [Lead-acid batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lead-acid_batteries "Lead-acid batteries") are still used in small budget applications.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

## Safety

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Safety")]

Most of the BESS systems are composed of securely sealed [battery packs](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battery_pack "Battery pack"), which are electronically monitored and replaced once their performance falls below a given threshold. Batteries suffer from cycle ageing, or deterioration caused by charge–discharge cycles. This deterioration is generally higher at [high charging rates](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battery_charger#C-rate "Battery charger") and higher [depth of discharge](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Depth_of_discharge "Depth of discharge"). This aging causes a loss of performance (capacity or voltage decrease), overheating, and may eventually lead to critical failure (electrolyte leaks, fire, explosion). Sometimes battery storage power stations are built with [flywheel storage power systems](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flywheel_storage_power_system "Flywheel storage power system") in order to conserve battery power.[[18]](#cite_note-18) Flywheels may handle rapid fluctuations better than older battery plants.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

BESS [warranties](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Warranties "Warranties") typically include lifetime limits on energy throughput, expressed as number of charge–discharge cycles.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Lead-acid based batteries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Lead-acid based batteries")]

Lead-acid batteries, as a first-generation technology, are generally used in older BESS systems.[[21]](#cite_note-21) Some examples are 1.6 MW peak, 1.0 MW continuous battery was commissioned in 1997.[[22]](#cite_note-22) Compared to modern rechargeable batteries, lead-acid batteries have relatively low [energy density](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Energy_density "Energy density"). Despite this, they are able to supply high [surge currents](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surge_current "Surge current"). However, non-sealed [lead-acid batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lead-acid_batteries "Lead-acid batteries") produce hydrogen and oxygen from the aqueous electrolyte when overcharged. The water has to be refilled regularly to avoid damage to the battery; and, the inflammable gases have to be vented out to avoid explosion risks. However, this maintenance has a cost, and recent batteries such as [Li-ion batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Li-ion_batteries "Li-ion batteries") do not have such an issue.

### Lithium-based batteries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lithium-based batteries")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8d/2010-_Grid_energy_storage_%28US%29.svg/250px-2010-_Grid_energy_storage_%28US%29.svg.png)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2010-_Grid_energy_storage_(US).svg)

The installed capacity of [lithium-ion grid battery storage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grid_energy_storage#Lithium-ion_batteries "Grid energy storage") in the US has grown steadily since the 2010s.[[23]](#cite_note-CanaryMedia_20260220-23)

[Lithium-ion batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium-ion_batteries "Lithium-ion batteries") offer a long lifespan with minimal maintenance, high energy density, and low [self-discharge](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Self-discharge "Self-discharge"),[[24]](#cite_note-24) which makes them ideal for modern utility-scale BESS applications.[[25]](#cite_note-EngineeringIntegrations2021-25)

A drawback of some types of lithium-ion batteries is fire safety, mostly ones containing cobalt.[[26]](#cite_note-epri2023-26) The number of BESS incidents has remained around 10–20 per year (mostly within the first 2–3 years of age), despite the large increase in number and size of BESS. Thus [failure rate](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Failure_rate "Failure rate") has decreased. Failures occurred mostly in controls and [balance of system](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balance_of_system "Balance of system"), while 11% occurred in cells.[[27]](#cite_note-epri2024-27)

Examples of BESS fire accidents include individual modules in 23 battery farms in [South Korea](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea") in 2017 to 2019,[[28]](#cite_note-28) a [Tesla Megapack](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tesla_Megapack "Tesla Megapack") in [Geelong](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geelong "Geelong"),[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) the fire and subsequent explosion of a battery module in [Arizona](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arizona "Arizona"),[[27]](#cite_note-epri2024-27) and the cooling liquid [short circuiting](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Short_circuit "Short circuit") incidents and fire at the [Moss Landing LG battery](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moss_Landing_Power_Plant#Vistra_Corp's_battery_storage_development "Moss Landing Power Plant").[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-fass-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33)

This resulted in more research in recent years for mitigation measures for fire safety.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

By 2024, the [lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium_iron_phosphate_battery "Lithium iron phosphate battery") has become another significant type for large storages due to the high availability of its components, [longer lifetime](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charge_cycle "Charge cycle") and higher safety compared to nickel-based Li-ion chemistries.[[35]](#cite_note-35) An LFP-based energy storage system that was installed in [Paiyun Lodge](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paiyun_Lodge "Paiyun Lodge") on [Mt. Jade (Yushan)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yu_Shan "Yu Shan") (the highest alpine lodge in [Taiwan](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan")) and operated since 2016 without a safety incident.[[36]](#cite_note-Batteries10(2024)202-36)

### Sodium-based batteries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sodium-based batteries")]

[Sodium-based batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sodium-ion_battery "Sodium-ion battery") are another BESS technology. Compared to lithium-ion batteries, sodium-ion batteries have somewhat lower cost, better safety characteristics, and similar power delivery characteristics, but lower energy density. Its [working principle](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium-ion_battery#Electrochemistry "Lithium-ion battery") and [cell construction](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium-ion_battery#Design "Lithium-ion battery") are similar, but use [sodium](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sodium "Sodium") instead of [lithium](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lithium "Lithium") as the [intercalating](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intercalation_(chemistry) "Intercalation (chemistry)") [ion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ion "Ion"). [Sodium–sulfur batteries](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sodium–sulfur_battery "Sodium–sulfur battery") can operate safely at high temperatures. Notable sodium battery producers with high safety claims include (non-exclusive) Altris AB, SgNaPlus and Tiamat. The largest BESS utilizing sodium-ion technology started operating in 2024 in Hubei province, boasts a capacity of 50 MW / 100 MWh.[[37]](#cite_note-37) In 2026, [CATL](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CATL "CATL") unveiled its modular TENER Sodium Energy Storage System, which offers 30 MWh storage capacity. It can deliver power for storage durations of 1, 2, 4, 6, and 8 hours, depending on the application. It operates at 65 [decibels](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decibels "Decibels"). It contracted with HyperStrong for a 60 gwh system.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

## Operating characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Operating characteristics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/Schwerin_20140923_wemag2.jpg/250px-Schwerin_20140923_wemag2.jpg)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Schwerin_20140923_wemag2.jpg)

Battery storage power plant at Schwerin (interior view 2014, modular rows of accumulators)

Since they do not have any mechanical parts, battery storage power plants offer extremely short control times and start times, as little as 10 ms.[[39]](#cite_note-39) They can therefore help dampen the fast oscillations that occur when electrical power networks are operated close to their maximum capacity or when grids suffer anomalies. These instabilities – fluctuations with periods of as much as 30 seconds – can produce peak swings of such amplitude that they can cause regional blackouts. Some of the parameters are voltage, frequency and phase. A properly sized battery storage power plant can efficiently counteract these oscillations; therefore, applications are found primarily in those regions where electrical power systems are operated at full capacity, leading to a risk of instability.[[40]](#cite_note-40) However, some batteries have insufficient control systems, failing during moderate disruptions they should have tolerated.[[41]](#cite_note-41) Batteries are also commonly used for [peak shaving](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peak_shaving "Peak shaving") for periods of up to a few hours.[[3]](#cite_note-GTM-3) A more recent use is strengthening transmission, as long power lines can be operated closer to their capacity when batteries handle the local difference between supply and demand.[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-43)

Storage plants can also be used in combination with an intermittent renewable energy source in [stand-alone power systems](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stand-alone_power_system "Stand-alone power system").[[44]](#cite_note-44)

## Largest grid batteries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Largest grid batteries")]

See also: [List of energy storage power plants](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_energy_storage_power_plants "List of energy storage power plants") and [List of largest power stations § Battery storage](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_largest_power_stations#Battery_storage "List of largest power stations")

### Operating

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Operating")]

## Market development and deployment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battery_energy_storage_system&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Market development and deployment")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/66/Average-battery-cell-price-b.png/250px-Average-battery-cell-price-b.png)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Average-battery-cell-price-b.png)

Lithium-ion battery cell prices by chemistry, 2014-2025

 [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/1991-_Lithium-ion_battery_prices_vs_installed_capacity.svg/250px-1991-_Lithium-ion_battery_prices_vs_installed_capacity.svg.png)](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:1991-_Lithium-ion_battery_prices_vs_installed_capacity.svg)

Since 1991, the cost of lithium-ion batteries has declined by 99%. On average, the price declined about 19% for each doubling of installed capacity.[[106]](#cite_note-OWIDbatteryPrice_202603-106)

2024 cost components of grid battery in Europe, % of 400 €/kWh system cost[[107]](#cite_note-inox2025-107)

1. Cells (35.0%)
2. [Power conversion](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Power_inverter "Power inverter") (15.0%)
3. [Balance of plant](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balance_of_plant "Balance of plant") (15.0%)
4. Infrastructure (20.0%)
5. Installation (15.0%)

By 2025, global grid battery power capacity was 267 GW with 610 GWh energy capacity,[[5]](#cite_note-tris2025-5) compared to the other major form of grid storage, [pumped-storage hydroelectricity](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pumped-storage_hydroelectricity "Pumped-storage hydroelectricity") with 200 GW power and 9000 GWh energy storage worldwide as of 2025 according to [International Hydropower Association](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Hydropower_Association "International Hydropower Association").[[108]](#cite_note-108) The battery market had thus surpassed the power generation capacity of pumped-storage, but remained far smaller in terms of energy capacity.

Relative to 2010, batteries and photovoltaics have followed roughly the same downward price curve due to [experience curve effects](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Experience_curve_effects "Experience curve effects").[[109]](#cite_note-rth-109) Cells are the major cost component, costing 30-40% of a full system.[[107]](#cite_note-inox2025-107) Batteries' rapid increase occurred as price drops,[[110]](#cite_note-110) with over 100 GW added (mostly LFP) in 2025, up from 10 GW in 2021. In 2025, solar power added was down to 6 times bigger than battery power added, as battery increased much faster than solar.[[111]](#cite_note-111) Average world system price was around $120/kWh in 2025.[[5]](#cite_note-tris2025-5)

The amount of batteries and their associated operational money flow became an investible [asset class](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asset_class "Asset class") by 2026, pooling several separate batteries into centrally controlled portfolios. This allows small developers with low funds to partner with groups of [institutional investors](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Institutional_investor "Institutional investor") without practical knowledge of the electricity business, to the benefit of both. This is a contrast to the usual single energy company approach of developing, owning and operating an electricity facility.[[112]](#cite_note-112)

By mid-2025, China passed 100 GW batteries (164 GW total storage)[[113]](#cite_note-vsCH-113) and added [capacity market payments](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electricity_market#Capacity_market "Electricity market").[[114]](#cite_note-114) As of May 2025, China’s cumulative BESS installations were reported at 106.9 GW and 240.3 GWh, with global battery storage deployment of nearly 9 GWh in April 2025.[[115]](#cite_note-115) At the end of 2024, China had 62 GW / 141 GWh of battery power stations.[[116]](#cite_note-116) In 2020, China added 1,557 MW to its battery storage capacity, while storage facilities for [photovoltaics](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photovoltaics "Photovoltaics") projects accounting for 27% of the capacity,[[117]](#cite_note-117) to the total 3,269 MW of electrochemical energy storage capacity.[[118]](#cite_note-118)

The United States installed 57.6 GWh in 2025,[[119]](#cite_note-119) and 12.3 GW / 37.1 GWh of batteries in 2024.[[120]](#cite_note-120) USA had 70 GWh production capacity in 2025, roughly corresponding to domestic market size.[[121]](#cite_note-121) In 2022, US capacity doubled to 9 GW / 25 GWh.[[122]](#cite_note-122) At the end of 2021, the capacity grew to 4,588 MW.[[123]](#cite_note-acp2021q4-123) The 2021 price of a 60 MW / 240 MWh (4-hour) battery installation in the United States was US$379/usable kWh, or US$292/nameplate kWh, a 13% drop from 2020.[[124]](#cite_note-124)[[125]](#cite_note-nrel-202111-125) In 2010, the United States had 59 MW of battery storage capacity from 7 battery power plants. This increased to 49 plants comprising 351 MW of capacity in 2015. In 2018, the capacity was 869 MW from 125 plants, capable of storing a maximum of 1,236 MWh of generated electricity. By the end of 2020, the battery storage capacity reached 1,756 MW.[[126]](#cite_note-126)[[127]](#cite_note-127) The US market for storage power plants in 2015 increased by 243% compared to 2014.[[128]](#cite_note-128)

In June 2024 the capacity was 4.6 GW of power and 5.9 GWh of energy in the United Kingdom.[[129]](#cite_note-129) In 2022, UK capacity grew by 800 MWh, ending at 2.4 GW / 2.6 GWh.[[130]](#cite_note-130) As of May 2021, 1.3 GW of battery storage was operating, with 16 GW of projects in the pipeline potentially deployable over the next few years.[[131]](#cite_note-solarpowerportal-20210519-131)

As of the end of 2024, Europe had reached 61 GWh of installed battery energy storage capacity, after adding 21 GWh that year. Germany and Italy each contributed approximately 6 GWh to this growth.[[132]](#cite_note-132) The average installation cost during 2024 ranged between €300 and €400 per kilowatt-hour.[[107]](#cite_note-inox2025-107) By comparison, Europe deployed 1.9 GW of new battery capacity in 2022.[[133]](#cite_note-133) Developments in Germany are closely monitored by [RWTH Aachen University](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/RWTH_Aachen_University "RWTH Aachen University") site *battery-charts.de*, reporting[[134]](#cite_note-134) in September 2025 15 GW and 22 GWh mostly in over 2 million home-based systems, while 1.84 Mio. registered Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) in Germany have an estimated energy capacity of over 115 GWh.

Japan’s energy sector has also undergone significant growth in renewable energy capacity. expanding by over 30% within five years, which has contributed to a sharp increase in demand for battery energy storage systems (BESS). More than half of the 2.4 GW of BESS capacity awarded in recent long-term low-carbon power auctions was allocated to foreign-owned companies or consortia. Projects approved in 2024 alone comprise more than 1.37 GW of power capacity and over 6.7 GWh of energy capacity.[[135]](#cite_note-135) The country’s Long-Term Decarbonization Power Source Auction supports BESS deployment by guaranteeing fixed cost recovery over a 20-year period. However, constraints such as limited price volatility and a price floor in Japan’s power market may limit investment returns for storage operators, signaling the need for further regulatory reform.[[136]](#cite_note-136)[[137]](#cite_note-137)

Worldwide in 2024, suppliers [CRRC](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CRRC "CRRC") had 8% market share, [Sungrow](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sungrow "Sungrow") 14%, and [Tesla Energy](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tesla_Energy "Tesla Energy") 15%.[[138]](#cite_note-138)

Some developers are also utilizing retired electric vehicle batteries to build second-life storage systems, with costs potentially 50% lower than those of new battery installations.[[139]](#cite_note-139) Nonetheless, due to the declining cost of new batteries, buyers of second-life systems may only be willing
